questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a testbench in the digital IC design verification flow?
options: To simulate the design's behavior and verify its functional correctness against specifications.
To define the physical dimensions and layout of the integrated circuit on a silicon wafer.
To optimize the gate-level netlist for power, performance, and area during synthesis.
To provide power and ground connections to the final packaged chip.
To measure the electrical characteristics of individual transistors after manufacturing.

questions.question: In the context of pipelined processor architectures, what typically defines a "data hazard"?
options: When two or more instructions attempt to access the same memory location simultaneously.
When an instruction requires the result of a previous instruction that has not yet completed its execution stage.
When a branch instruction misprediction causes the pipeline to fetch incorrect instructions.
When multiple instructions in different pipeline stages compete for the same hardware resource (e.g., an ALU).
When the instruction cache misses, causing a stall while the instruction is fetched from main memory.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a "power grid" in the physical layout of a complex Integrated Circuit (IC)?
options: To distribute clock signals uniformly across the entire chip.
To provide a robust, low-resistance network for distributing VDD (power) and GND (ground) to all active circuit elements.
To route critical high-speed data paths between different functional blocks.
To implement on-chip voltage regulation and power management units.
To serve as a shield against electromagnetic interference from external sources.

questions.question: In advanced digital IC design, particularly for achieving lower power consumption and managing thermal dissipation, what is "voltage scaling" primarily used for?
options: To reduce the physical size of individual transistors on the silicon die.
To increase the logic threshold voltage (Vt) of MOSFETs for better noise immunity.
To lower the supply voltage (VDD) to significantly reduce dynamic and static power consumption.
To adjust the operating frequency of the circuit to meet specific performance targets.
To convert analog input signals into digital values for internal processing.

questions.question: What is "crosstalk" in the context of digital IC interconnects, and why is it a significant concern at advanced technology nodes?
options: It refers to the unintended electrical coupling between adjacent signal lines, where a switching signal on one line can induce noise or errors on another, becoming more severe as wires get closer.
It describes the physical intersection of different metal layers in the chip layout, causing increased capacitance.
It is a technique for monitoring the resistance and capacitance of long interconnect wires during operation.
It refers to the process of transmitting data between different chips using shared communication channels.
It is the delay incurred when a signal needs to pass through multiple clock domains on a System-on-Chip (SoC).
